pick.rationale: This Sweet 16 matchup features two elite defensive teams with Iowa State ranking 6th and Virginia 14th in adjusted defensive efficiency, but the Cyclones hold a slight analytical edge as a top-6 team in KenPom compared to Virginia's 13th. Iowa State's 27-7 record came against the 42nd-toughest schedule in the Big 12, and they proved their elite status by taking #3 Arizona to a buzzer-beater in the Big 12 semifinals after dominating Texas Tech 75-53. Virginia's shot-blocking duo of Ugonna Onyenso and Johann Grünloh presents a unique challenge, but Iowa State's slightly superior offense (21st vs 28th in offensive rating) and overall efficiency margin give them a narrow advantage. The Cyclones' concerning free-throw struggles (last in the Big 12) could keep this competitive, but their defensive prowess and proven ability to compete with elite competition makes them the slight favorite in this evenly-matched showdown.
